chapter: Chapter: Electricity – Magnetic and Heating Effects

questions.question: What is electric current?
questions.explanation: Electric current is the flow of electric charges through a conductor.

questions.question: Which device provides electrical energy in a circuit?
questions.explanation: A battery supplies electrical energy to the circuit.

questions.question: What is a closed circuit?

options.A: A circuit with a broken path
options.C: A circuit with a complete path for current

questions.explanation: A closed circuit allows electric current to flow because the path is complete.

questions.question: What is an open circuit?

options.A: A circuit with a complete path
options.B: A circuit with a broken path

questions.explanation: In an open circuit the path is broken and current cannot flow.

questions.question: Which material is commonly used for electrical wires?
questions.explanation: Copper is a good conductor of electricity and commonly used for wires.

questions.question: Which device is used to control the flow of electric current in a circuit?
questions.explanation: A switch opens or closes the circuit to control current flow.

questions.question: What happens to a bulb in a closed circuit?
questions.explanation: In a closed circuit current flows and the bulb glows.

questions.question: What is the heating effect of electric current?

options.B: Production of heat when current flows

questions.explanation: Electric current flowing through a conductor produces heat.

questions.question: Which appliance uses the heating effect of current?
questions.explanation: Electric irons use the heating effect of electric current.

questions.question: What is an electric fuse?
questions.explanation: A fuse protects circuits by melting when excess current flows.

questions.question: Why does a fuse melt?
questions.explanation: Excess current causes the fuse wire to heat and melt.

questions.question: What is the magnetic effect of electric current?

options.C: Production of magnetic field around a conductor

questions.explanation: A current-carrying conductor produces a magnetic field.

questions.question: Which instrument shows the magnetic effect of current?
questions.explanation: A compass needle deflects when near a current-carrying wire.

questions.question: What is an electromagnet?

options.B: Temporary magnet produced by electric current

questions.explanation: An electromagnet is produced when current flows through a coil.

questions.question: Which material is usually used as the core of an electromagnet?
questions.explanation: Soft iron is commonly used as the core of an electromagnet.

questions.question: What happens when current stops flowing in an electromagnet?
questions.explanation: Electromagnets work only when current flows.

questions.question: Which device commonly uses an electromagnet?
questions.explanation: Electric bells use electromagnets to produce sound.

questions.question: Which effect of current is used in electric bells?
questions.explanation: Electric bells work using electromagnets created by current.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of insulation around wires?
questions.explanation: Insulation protects people from electric shocks.

questions.question: Which part of an electric circuit uses electrical energy to produce light?
questions.explanation: A bulb converts electrical energy into light energy.

questions.question: Which effect of electric current is used in electric heaters?
questions.explanation: Electric heaters use the heating effect of current.

questions.question: Which direction does electric current flow in a simple circuit?

options.A: From battery through wires to device

questions.explanation: Current flows from the battery through wires to the device in a closed circuit.

questions.question: Which effect of electric current produces magnetism?
questions.explanation: Electric current produces a magnetic field around a conductor.

questions.question: What happens when a switch is turned OFF?
questions.explanation: Turning OFF the switch breaks the circuit.

questions.question: Which effect of current is responsible for electromagnets?
questions.explanation: Electromagnets work due to the magnetic effect of electric current.
